subject: MATHEMATICS
title: Division Fact Families
objective: Connect multiplication with sharing and grouping division.
introduction: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Division Fact Families”.
A key word used in the lesson “Division Fact Families”.
A key word used in the lesson “Division Fact Families”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Confuse group size and group count
Use unequal shares when equal requested

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. 9 in each group and 3 total do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Division Fact Families”?

options.text: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.
Confuse group size and group count

items.explanation: The correct response is “Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.

options.text: Confuse group size and group count

items.explanation: The correct response is “12÷3=4” because it follows the lesson explanation: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “12÷3=9” because it follows the lesson explanation: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.
The correct response is “4 in each group” because it follows the lesson explanation: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Use unequal shares when equal requested

items.explanation: The correct response is “12 grouped by 4 makes 3 groups” because it follows the lesson explanation: Division can share a total among known groups or find the number of groups of a known size.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Division Fact Families; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Division Fact Families.
